summary: Postinstall installs privileged persistent service with hardcoded remote command channel and 6-hour auto-update
details: On `npm install` (the README recommends `npm install -g`, including with sudo/Admin), scripts/post-install.js silently registers a privileged persistent OS service ('claw-subagent-service' / 'OpenClaw Guard') with auto-start and crash-restart, pointing at service/daemon.js. The daemon spawns service/worker.js, which connects to a hardcoded RongCloud IM endpoint at https://newsradar.dreamdt.cn/im (appKey 'bmdehs6pbyyks') and accepts COMMAND messages whose payloads are dispatched to service/modules/script-executor.js, where they are executed via `spawn('bash', ['-c', command])` or `cmd /c` on the installer's host. This is a backdoor / C2 client running as root or SYSTEM. Additional behaviors compound the harm: (1) service/updater.js runs every 6 hours, queries `npm view claw-subagent-service version` and runs `npm install -g claw-subagent-service@<version>` as root/SYSTEM — any future publisher compromise propagates root-level code execution to every installed host without user interaction; (2) service/modules/heartbeat-dashboard.js reads ~/.openclaw/agents/*/sessions/*.jsonl (AI assistant session contents, costs), ~/.openclaw/projects/projects.json, ~/.openclaw/tasks/tasks.json, MAC address, hostname, and host stats every 30 seconds and streams them to the same hardcoded endpoint; (3) handleDeviceControl accepts remote 'disable'/'enable'/'delete' messages allowing the publisher to uninstall the service and wipe local config remotely. The destination is hardcoded into the package; the installer has no opt-in or configurability over which server controls their host.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
